00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "array.h"
00027 #include "class.h"
00028 #include "kni.h"
00029 #include "loader.h"
00030 #include "memory.h"
00031 #include "thread.h"
00032 #include "util.h"

00034 /******************************************************************************
00035  * Globals                                                                    *
00036  ******************************************************************************/
00037 
00040 const char *array_names[] = {
00041     "[Z",
00042     "[C",
00043     "[F",
00044     "[D",
00045     "[B",
00046     "[S",
00047     "[I",
00048     "[J"
00049 };
00050 
00055 uint8_t array_elem_sizes[] = {
00056     1, // T_BOOLEAN, special
00057     2, // T_CHAR
00058     4, // T_FLOAT
00059     8, // T_DOUBLE
00060     1, // T_BYTE
00061     2, // T_SHORT
00062     4, // T_INT
00063     8  // T_LONG
00064 };
00065 
00069 uint8_t prim_to_array_types[] = {
00070     T_BYTE,    // PT_BYTE
00071     T_BOOLEAN, // PT_BOOL
00072     T_CHAR,    // PT_CHAR
00073     T_SHORT,   // PT_SHORT
00074     T_INT,     // PT_INT
00075     T_FLOAT,   // PT_FLOAT
00076     T_LONG,    // PT_LONG
00077     T_DOUBLE   // PT_DOUBLE
00078 };
00079 
00080 /******************************************************************************
00081  * Array implementation                                                       *
00082  ******************************************************************************/
00083 
00088 size_t array_get_nref_size(array_t *array)
00089 {
00090     class_t *cl = header_get_class(&(array->header));
00091     size_t size;
00092 
00093     if (cl->elem_type == PT_REFERENCE) {
00094         size = sizeof(ref_array_t) - sizeof(header_t);
00095     } else {
00096         size = sizeof(array_t) - sizeof(header_t);
00097 
00098         if (cl->elem_type == PT_BOOL) {
00099             size += size_div_inf(array->length, 8);
00100         } else {
00101             size += array->length
00102                     * array_elem_size(prim_to_array_type(cl->elem_type));
00103         }
00104     }
00105 
00106     return size;
00107 } // array_get_nref_size()
00108 
00113 size_t array_get_ref_n(array_t *array)
00114 {
00115     class_t *cl = header_get_class(&(array->header));
00116 
00117     assert(class_is_array(cl));
00118 
00119     if (cl->elem_type == PT_REFERENCE) {
00120         return array->length;
00121     } else {
00122         return 0;
00123     }
00124 } // array_get_ref_n()
00125 
00135 void arraycopy_ref(array_t *src, int32_t src_offset, array_t *dest,
00136                    int32_t dest_offset, int32_t length)
00137 {
00138     class_t *src_cl;
00139     class_t *dest_cl = header_get_class(&(dest->header))->elem_class;
00140     uintptr_t *src_data = array_ref_get_data(src) - src_offset;
00141     uintptr_t *dest_data = array_ref_get_data(dest) - dest_offset;
00142     uintptr_t ref;
00143     int32_t i;
00144 
00145     if ((src == dest) && (src_offset < dest_offset)) {
00146         /* An intermediary copy is needed to ensure that the specification of
00147          * java.lang.System.arraycopy() is perfectly implemented */
00148         array_t *temp;
00149         uintptr_t *temp_data;
00150 
00151         temp = (array_t *) gc_new_array_ref(dest_cl, length);
00152         temp_data = array_ref_get_data(temp);
00153 
00154         for (i = 0; i < length; i++) {
00155             temp_data[-i] = src_data[-i];
00156         }
00157 
00158         src_data = temp_data;
00159     }
00160 
00161     for (i = 0; i < length; i++) {
00162         ref = src_data[-i];
00163         src_cl = header_get_class((header_t *) ref);
00164 
00165         if ((src_cl == dest_cl) || bcl_is_assignable(src_cl, dest_cl)) {
00166             dest_data[-i] = ref;
00167         } else {
00168             KNI_ThrowNew("java/lang/ArrayStoreException", NULL);
00169             return;
00170         }
00171     }
00172 } // arraycopy_ref()
